notes: Acquired by Compaq for $9.6B in June 1998. Compaq acquired by HP in 2002. Alpha processor IP sold to Intel in 2001; support ended 2007.

Founded 1968; pre-billing communication systems for telcos. Note: this CGI is distinct from CGI Group Inc. (Canadian IT services firm). The telecom CGI was absorbed into the telco BSS vendor market. CGI Group Inc. remains active.
UK-based IT services firm with SMS messaging capability. European SMS market leader. Merged with Logica in 2002 to form LogicaCMG. Logica was acquired by CGI Group Inc. in 2012.
DIGITAL/Dialogic partnership established 1994. Dialogic acquired by Intel for ~$780M in 1999. See Study 1 for full Dialogic history.
Provider of Arbor/BP customer care and billing software. Completed Alpha port December 1996. Acquired by Lucent Technologies for $1.48B in 1999. Later Kenan assets acquired by Amdocs in 2015.
Newbridge Networks affiliate; provided IP voice and IP fax gateway solutions. Acquired by Nokia for approximately $90M in December 1998. Enabled Nokia to enter the IP telephony/gateway market.
Vienna Systems was a Newbridge affiliate. Newbridge Networks acquired by Alcatel for $7.1B in 2000.
DIGITAL customer reference cited in study. Nortel filed for bankruptcy in 2009; patent portfolio sold to Rockstar Consortium (Apple, Microsoft et al.) for $4.5B.
DIGITAL customer reference. Merged with Lucent Technologies in 2006; Nokia acquired Alcatel-Lucent in 2016.
